headline: 'Gaza has become a diplomatic, political and military black hole'
description: Donald Trump's promises for the territory, devastated after two years of war, have continued to be proven wrong in the months following the ceasefire with Israel, writes Le Monde columnist Gilles Paris.

headline: Air France and Airbus found guilty on appeal of involuntary manslaughter in 2009 Rio-Paris crash
NGOs sue French state over failure to tackle 'forever chemicals'
Ben-Gvir's video of European activists exhibits his policy of ill-treating Palestinian prisoners
description: Israel's far-right National Security Minister sparked international outcry and was even rebuked by the US ambassador and Benjamin Netanyahu after he posted footage of European activists held with heads to the ground and hands bound.

headline: Trump tightens grip on Republican Party in US primaries
description: Candidates backed by Donald Trump for the Republican Party nomination in the November midterm elections benefit from the mobilization of the MAGA base, which remains loyal to the billionaire despite his declining popularity.

headline: These winemakers believe melody makes better wine: 'Right after bottling, we play Beethoven on a loop'
description: In the vineyards, while fermenting or even during aging, some winemakers use various melodies that they believe stimulate the vines, protect them from disease or intensify the wine's aromas.

headline: JR covers Pont-Neuf bridge in Paris with giant inflatable cave
description: The temporary installation 'La Caverne du Pont-Neuf' has been set up on the Paris landmark since Thursday. The site will be open to the public free of charge, 24 hours a day, for three weeks from June 6.

'Team Musul': The French youths convicted of attempting to join IS in Mozambique
description: Six young French nationals were convicted in Paris for planning to migrate to Mozambique together and join the IS-affiliated jihadist group al-Shabab in Cabo Delgado province, where it has been active since 2017.

headline: In a tsunami of data leaks, French society remains vulnerable and authorities powerless
description: France is experiencing a record number of data breaches. Despite the damage caused by these hacks, the level of cybersecurity in businesses and government agencies remains inadequate.

headline: Cuba's crackdown on dissidents continues, despite US pressure
description: In an effort to show good faith to Washington, Havana recently pardoned more than 2,000 detainees. However, most of those who benefited were ordinary delinquents. The Cuban authorities continue to arrest dissidents, bringing the number of political prisoners to 1,260, a record high.
